title: Design webhook/trigger support for external service integration
markdown:
## Goal

Design a webhook and trigger system that allows B3nd nodes to notify external services when specific URI patterns receive new data, enabling integration with third-party systems (e.g., Slack notifications, CI/CD pipelines, analytics ingestion) without requiring those systems to poll the node.

## Background

Today, the only way to react to data changes on a B3nd node is through `connect()` polling (in `libs/b3nd-listener/mod.ts`) or by building a custom compose `Processor` that side-effects on receive. There is no standard mechanism for a node operator to say "when anything is written to `mutable://invoices/*`, POST the payload to `https://my-erp.example.com/webhook`". The `respondTo` and `connect` primitives in `libs/b3nd-listener/mod.ts` handle node-to-node communication but not node-to-external-HTTP.

The compose system (`libs/b3nd-compose/`) provides `Processor`, `when()`, `parallel()`, and `pipe()` combinators that could host webhook dispatch as a processor in the pipeline. The HTTP server (`libs/b3nd-servers/http.ts`) has a `configure()` method that accepts a `Node` or `client`, but no hook points for outbound notifications.

Key design questions:
- Should webhooks be registered via API calls, config files, or on-chain URIs?
- How to handle delivery failures (retry, dead-letter, circuit breaker)?
- Should payloads include the full data or just a notification with the URI?
- How does this interact with encrypted data (can a webhook see plaintext)?
- What authentication does the webhook target require (HMAC signatures, bearer tokens)?

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] RFC document created at `docs/design/webhooks.md` covering the full design
- [ ] Design specifies webhook registration mechanism (how operators define triggers)
- [ ] Design specifies webhook payload format (what gets sent to the target URL)
- [ ] Design specifies delivery guarantees (at-least-once, retry policy, dead-letter queue)
- [ ] Design specifies authentication for outbound requests (HMAC signing, custom headers)
- [ ] Design specifies how webhooks compose with the existing `Processor` pipeline
- [ ] Design addresses encrypted data handling (webhook sees ciphertext vs plaintext)
- [ ] Design includes URI pattern matching rules (glob, prefix, exact)
- [ ] Design includes a `WebhookProcessor` type signature compatible with `libs/b3nd-compose/types.ts`
- [ ] Design includes at least two sequence diagrams (happy path, delivery failure)
- [ ] Design documents security considerations (SSRF prevention, URL allowlisting)

## Implementation Guide

1. Read `libs/b3nd-compose/types.ts` to understand the `Processor` type and how combinators like `when()` and `parallel()` work.

2. Read `libs/b3nd-listener/mod.ts` to understand how `respondTo()` wraps handlers as processors and how the decrypt-call-encrypt cycle works. Note how this pattern could inform webhook dispatch.

3. Read `libs/b3nd-servers/http.ts` to understand the `httpServer()` function, its `configure()` method, and how routes are wired. Consider where webhook management endpoints would live.

4. Read `libs/b3nd-core/types.ts` to understand `Message`, `ReceiveResult`, and `NodeProtocolInterface` -- the primitives that webhooks would observe.

5. Create `docs/design/webhooks.md` with the following structure:
   - **Overview**: What webhooks solve and who benefits
   - **Registration**: How webhook subscriptions are created (API endpoint, config, or URI-based)
   - **Matching**: URI pattern syntax (prefix match, glob, regex) and how patterns are evaluated
   - **Payload format**: JSON structure sent to target URLs, including metadata (uri, timestamp, node identity)
   - **Delivery**: HTTP POST semantics, retry policy (exponential backoff, max attempts), timeout, dead-letter
   - **Authentication**: HMAC-SHA256 signature header, optional bearer token, custom headers
   - **Compose integration**: How `WebhookProcessor` fits into `parallel()` or `pipe()` chains
   - **Encryption handling**: Webhooks only see ciphertext for encrypted URIs; operators can opt in to plaintext if they hold the key
   - **Security**: SSRF prevention (private IP blocking, URL allowlist), rate limiting outbound calls
   - **Type signatures**: Draft TypeScript interfaces for `WebhookConfig`, `WebhookSubscription`, `WebhookProcessor`
   - **Sequence diagrams**: Mermaid diagrams for happy path and failure/retry path
   - **Open questions**: List remaining decisions for implementation phase

## Files to Create/Modify

- `docs/design/webhooks.md` -- New RFC document with full webhook/trigger design

## Testing

This is a design-only story. Verification is via review of the RFC document:
- All acceptance criteria sections are present and substantive
- Type signatures are valid TypeScript
- Sequence diagrams render correctly in Mermaid
- Security considerations are thorough

## Dependencies

No prerequisites. This is a standalone design document. Implementation stories will depend on this design being approved.
tasksExcerpt:
- [ ] Design webhook/trigger support for external service integration
- [ ] Design "durable" storage tier or SLA mechanism for compliance-sensitive use cases
- [ ] Design multi-user access control beyond shared keypairs (role-based access to a namespace)
- [ ] Design offline-first sync framework (local client ↔ remote client reconciliation)

---

## E. Infrastructure & DevOps
